old_claim.note: Compass provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained between Lateen Sail & Caravel and Compass; Caravel hull and sail design is not caused by the magnetic compass, even though compass navigation helped later voyages.

source_shape.source_locator: Article describes the caravel as a light sailing ship of the 15th to 17th centuries, developed by the Portuguese for African coastal exploration and able to sail to windward.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Caravel hull and sail design is not caused by the magnetic compass, even though compass navigation helped later voyages.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports the scoped Lateen Sail & Caravel claim or adjacent chronology without requiring Compass as a prerequisite.

ontology_before: Lateen Sail & Caravel treated Compass as a direct prerequisite.
ontology_after: Lateen Sail & Caravel no longer directly depends on Compass; the dependency boundary is narrowed to operationally necessary predecessors.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: lateen_sail_caravel must not directly depend on compass unless a narrower source-backed node scope is introduced.
would_reject_if: A source shows lateen_sail_caravel is intentionally scoped only to cases that require compass.
The graph splits lateen_sail_caravel and reserves this id for a narrower technology that directly depends on compass.
A stronger source demonstrates that compass is chronologically and causally necessary for lateen_sail_caravel.
short_reason: Caravel hull and sail design is not caused by the magnetic compass, even though compass navigation helped later voyages.
